op.text: On March 14, 2025, Dana scattered Mom's ashes at Beacon Rock in the Columbia River Gorge. Dana went alone. Beacon Rock was 'ours' — Mom used to take Dana there as a kid, and on the way up taught Dana to read cloud shapes: 'Not just "that one's a rabbit" — she had this whole taxonomy, what a mare's tail sky meant, when the anvil clouds meant get off the water.' Dana said: 'I stood at the top and the sky was doing exactly the kind of high thin herringbone she loved, and I let her go into the wind.' The urn had been in Dana's closet for months; Dana 'couldn't even look at that side of the closet.' Dana picked up a fir cone from the trail on the way down; it's on the windowsill above the kitchen sink now. Sam had soup waiting when Dana got home. Dana slept eleven hours. (2025-03-18)
[as of 2025-03-18] In a cutback week in marathon training (as of 2025-03-18)
